Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Op-Ed: Seating architecture must evolve to accommodate power, IFEC

Please enjoy this Op-Ed contribution from Unum VP Business Development, Alan McInnes Moving beyond the traditional considerations of structure, comfort and certification, aircraft seating is a critical integration point for power, in-flight entertainment (IFE) and connectivity systems that...

Aircraft seating is evolving from a passive structure into an active platform for power distribution and connectivity, requiring manufacturers to redesign seat architecture for integrated IFEC systems.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

The One Metric That Matters Most In Premium Economy

Premium economy has grown significantly in popularity over the past decade.

Premium economy's expansion reflects airlines' ability to capture higher margins from cost-conscious business travelers, directly impacting fleet economics and revenue optimization strategies.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Southwest Airlines Adds 42 New Nonstop Routes This Spring [Map]

They include four new cities, with one in particular being historic...

Southwest's network expansion into four new cities signals the carrier's confidence in post-pandemic leisure demand recovery and competitive positioning against larger carriers in regional markets.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Why Is Airbus Creating A New A220?

Airbus is moving ahead with plans for an updated A220.

Airbus is modernizing the A220 to compete with Boeing's 737 MAX and extend its market dominance in the 130-160 seat segment.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Mar 29, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document their rationale for accepting residual risk after implementing controls, creating accountability trails that regulators increasingly scrutinize during audits.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Military Mar 29, 2026

6 Aircraft That Represent The Future Of Air Combat

Discover six aircraft redefining modern air warfare, from stealth fighters to missile trucks, shaping the balance of air power in 2026 and beyond.

Next-generation combat aircraft emphasize multi-role capabilities and standoff weapons deployment, reducing pilot risk while extending operational range and lethality.

See Simple Flying →

General Aviation Mar 29, 2026

Former United Boeing 747 Literally Wedged Between Two Seattle Skyscrapers

A decommissioned Boeing 747 will be suspended between two high-rise apartment buildings in downtown Seattle as part of an art project. This cool installation is set to hang around 14 feet (4.2 meters) above the ground between the two towers of the WB 1200 Project, located at 1200 Stewart Street in...

Retired widebody aircraft are increasingly repurposed for architectural art installations, demonstrating alternative end-of-life strategies beyond traditional scrapyard recycling.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 29, 2026

Breaking The ICE: Passenger Hands Out Vodka Shots As Federal Agents Man TSA Lines

While ICE agents are handing out water across airports, one passenger at a US airport is pouring out vodka shots for fellow passengers!

Security theater moments like vodka distribution during federal presence highlight gaps between strict TSA screening protocols and actual airport enforcement consistency.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Mar 29, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Organizations with strong psychological safety see 3-5x higher safety event reporting rates, enabling proactive hazard identification before incidents occur.

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

India inaugurates Noida International Airport, Delhi’s second gateway

India opened the first phase of Noida International Airport (DXN) in Jewar, Uttar Pradesh, on March 28, 2026,…...

Delhi's second airport capacity expansion positions India to capture growing passenger demand without network congestion at existing hubs.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 29, 2026

Rare US "Eye In The Sky" Command Plane Destroyed In Iran Strike On Saudi Air Base

The US has just a handful of E-3 Sentry's in mission-capable condition.

Loss of one E-3 Sentry significantly reduces US airborne early warning capacity in the Middle East, as the fleet is already critically limited in operational availability.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 29, 2026

USAF E-3 Sentry AWACS damaged in Iranian strike on Saudi air base

An Iranian missile and drone attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia on March 27, 2026,…...

Loss of an AWACS platform reduces regional air defense coordination capabilities and demonstrates vulnerability of fixed military installations to coordinated multi-vector attacks.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Mar 29, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure at RNO requires strict adherence to specific procedures due to terrain and airspace constraints that differ significantly from standard departures.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Just 2 Hours: The World's Shortest Airbus A380 Route Gets Even More Flights

Two different carriers fly their superjumbos on this corridor.

Competitive capacity dumping on ultra-short A380 routes signals carriers prioritizing market share over unit economics on short-haul superjumbo operations.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

7 Longest Nonstop Flights In The World In 2026

Four out of the seven use the Boeing 787.

The Boeing 787's superior efficiency dominates ultra-long-haul operations, with over half of the world's longest nonstop routes now relying on this aircraft type.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 29, 2026

‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings

While NASA outlined plans to increase the cadence of robotic lunar lander missions at a recent event, it said little about accelerating work on crewed lunar landers.

NASA's focus on accelerating robotic lunar missions may indicate a strategic pivot toward proving landing technologies before committing resources to crewed lander development.

See SpaceNews →

▶
Safety Mar 29, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway incursion risks at major hubs like LAX often stem from pilot confusion during standard exit procedures, requiring enhanced briefings and taxiway clarity.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Safety Mar 29, 2026

"The Runway Wasn't Clear": Southwest 737 Touches Down Before Abrupt Burbank Go-Around

Reportedly, the pilot announced to the passengers that the runway was not clear when the aircraft touched down, resulting in a go-around.

Runway incursion risks persist at congested airports, requiring heightened coordination between air traffic control and flight crews during approach phases.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 29, 2026

USAF A-10 strike on Iraqi military base kills seven soldiers

An airstrike attributed to a US Air Force A-10 Thunderbolt II hit a military clinic at the Habbaniyah…...

Civilian casualty incidents involving close-air-support platforms highlight ongoing challenges in target discrimination within complex conflict environments.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 28, 2026

Northrop Grumman Testbed With AIM-260-Like Nose Spotted Flying Alongside Raytheon’s VOODOO 1 at Eglin AFB

The two testbeds, known by their callsigns SCAN 06 and VOODOO 1, carried out concurrent operations in Eglin’s highly instrumented airspace. For three consecutive days, on Mar. 24, 25 and 26, two testbed aircraft, the Northrop Grumman CRJ-700 carrying the U.S. registration code N806X and typically...

Concurrent testing of advanced air-to-air missile systems by competing defense contractors suggests accelerated development timelines for next-generation beyond-visual-range combat capabilities.

See The Aviationist →

Industry Mar 29, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all three domains—airlines, airports, and flight operations—offers a near-term pathway to accommodate projected air traffic growth without proportional environmental impact increases.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 28, 2026

Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission

Starfish Space has changed plans for a docking demonstration mission after its original partner backed out.

Starfish Space's pivot to a new partner signals the orbital servicing sector's growing momentum despite setbacks, as multiple companies compete to validate docking technologies critical for on-orbit refueling and satellite maintenance.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 28, 2026

Germany Considering MQ-28A Ghost Bat CCA, Says Defense Minister

German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius said the MQ-28 Ghost Bat is among the uncrewed aircraft being evaluated to support the Eurofighter Typhoon fighter fleet. Germany’s defense minister Boris Pistorius said during his visit to Australia on Mar. 27, 2026, that Berlin is considering Boeing Defence...

Germany's evaluation of the MQ-28 Ghost Bat signals NATO's shift toward integrating autonomous combat aircraft with existing fighter fleets for collaborative warfare capabilities.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 28, 2026

USMC F-35Cs and USS Tripoli ARG Arrive in the Middle East

A month after the start of the hostilities, the U.S. Marine Corps is increasing its presence in the Middle East with the arrival of a F-35C squadron and the USS Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group. Mar. 28, 2026, marks a month since the U.S. and Israel launched Operations Epic Fury and Roaring Lion,...

The deployment demonstrates the F-35C's operational readiness in contested environments and reinforces naval aviation's critical role in power projection during active regional conflicts.

See The Aviationist →

Drones Mar 28, 2026

Robinson Unmanned

Robinson Unmanned (formerly Ascent AeroSystems) is the uncrewed aircraft systems (UAS) business unit of Robinson...

Robinson's pivot to dedicated UAS operations signals traditional helicopter manufacturers' strategic shift toward autonomous systems as core business lines.

See sUAS News →

Drones Mar 28, 2026

Quantum Systems Strengthens Ukraine’s Air Defence Capabilities with 15,000 Interceptor Contract and WIY Drones Investment

In alignment with the defence priorities set out by the Ukrainian Government, Quantum Systems has...

Commercial drone manufacturer's shift toward military production demonstrates how civilian UAS suppliers are becoming critical infrastructure partners in modern conflict scenarios.

See sUAS News →

Regulatory Mar 28, 2026

Singapore delays SAF levy rollout amid Middle East conflict

As the war in Iran continues to roil world oil markets, Singapore has delayed the rollout of its…...

Geopolitical oil market volatility is forcing governments to recalibrate aviation tax implementation timelines, signaling broader economic uncertainty for airline operations.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 28, 2026

Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC

The four Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in Florida March 27 for final preparations ahead of a launch still scheduled as soon as April 1.

Artemis 2's compressed pre-launch timeline demonstrates NASA's accelerated return-to-moon cadence, with only days between crew arrival and a potential April liftoff.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 27, 2026

[Updated] Iranian Attack on Prince Sultan Air Base Reportedly Struck U.S. KC-135s, E-3 Sentry

Satellite images show signs of an attack at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia, with The Wall Street Journal now reporting that an Iranian missile damaged “several U.S. refueling aircraft.” Satellite images emerged on Mar. 27, 2026, appeared to show signs of damage on an apron used by U.S....

Damage to KC-135 tankers reduces critical air-refueling capacity for U.S. operations in the Middle East, potentially constraining extended-range missions across the region.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

I Am Artemis: Erik Richards

Listen to this audio excerpt from Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager: For Erik Richards, supporting NASA’s first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon and back is the culmination of a career spent helping spacecraft communicate with Earth. Like many kids who grew up at the height of the...

Ground communication infrastructure, managed by specialized mission experts like Richards, is critical enabling technology that directly determines mission success for lunar exploration programs.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon

NASA has awarded Intuitive Machines of Houston, $180.4 million to deliver NASA-funded science and technology to the lunar surface as part of the agency’s CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) initiative and Artemis program. This lunar delivery, which includes seven payloads — five of them NASA’s...

NASA's $180.4M contract with Intuitive Machines represents a strategic shift toward leveraging commercial partners for lunar logistics, reducing direct agency reliance while advancing Artemis infrastructure development.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories

Category I: NASA Environmental Quality Award Recognizes excellence in environmental management and planning, including stewardship of natural and cultural resources. This category highlights achievements in compliance, conservation, remediation, communication, and environmental information...

NASA's environmental awards framework emphasizes integrated stewardship across compliance, conservation, and remediation, signaling aerospace's growing accountability for environmental management beyond operational efficiency.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

EASA warns non-airworthy stolen engine parts could re-enter the market

The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) is warning airlines, maintenance providers and parts buyers to be on…...

Stolen engine components from aircraft graveyards are being refurbished and resold through gray markets, creating undetectable airworthiness risks that bypass standard certification chains.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members

March 27, 2026 – Washington, D.C.—The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) is pleased to welcome Astrolab and Zeno Power, two innovative companies advancing planetary mobility and reliable energy in extreme environments. […]...

The CSF's expansion to include lunar mobility and power solutions providers signals aviation industry convergence with space infrastructure development, creating potential supply chain opportunities for aerospace suppliers.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions

MUNICH — For more than a year, questions have swirled about Artemis changes and the Gateway’s role in U.S. lunar ambitions. Following NASA’s March 24 decision to halt work on Gateway, the lunar-orbiting station that had been intended to support astronauts before and after lunar surface missions,...

ESA's June decision on Gateway funding will determine whether Europe maintains strategic influence in lunar exploration or becomes a minor contributor to NASA's revised Artemis architecture.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 27, 2026

Gallery: Luke Days 2026

A glimpse back at last weekend's air show at Luke Air Force Base in Arizona.

Luke Days showcases the F-35 Lightning II and other advanced fighter platforms, providing critical public engagement for the largest F-35 training hub in the world.
